summary: A fresh EDGAR/StockTitan sweep this cycle confirms NO new July public offering on any held name, so the book's biggest risk stays the standing dilution/distribution overhang on the three elevated holdings -- UUUU (undrawn $300M ATM + escalating Chalmers Form 144 selling, 200k 7/2 + 300k 7/9) and NTSK (live post-lockup Lightspeed distribution) -- compounded by WRAP's badly desynced price cache (broker_disagrees 38.9%) whose phantom-sell path is now gated by fix a5484b6; WRAP's only new securities item is a small $2M Frenel securities-purchase agreement (7/13 8-K) that intel's 14:06 alert did not surface.

disagreements: UUUU: intel frames the Bhappu/Hansen insider BUYS prominently ('no boost'); the Chalmers Form 144 SALE stack (200k 7/2 + 300k 7/9 via RBC, ~$10M+) outweighs the ~$1M of buying by size, so the insider signal behind the score is net-NEGATIVE -- reinforces the elevated grade.

NTSK: intel narrates the ICONIQ/director BUYS as the story; the offsetting Lightspeed VC sales (7/9) plus the 7/8 Form 144 (1.75M Class A) are a live post-lockup distribution overhang -- the net float pressure is why this stays elevated despite the +$7.72 gain.

WRAP: intel's 14:06 alert calls WRAP news just 'the 7/15 Wraptor MX prototype PR (incremental, not a contract)' and omits the 7/13 8-K -- a $2M Frenel securities-purchase agreement, i.e. an actual (small) share issuance plus US/NATO exclusive rights. Minor dilution but a real securities event intel did not surface.

Overtrading (carried): radar shows weekly buy budget 9/4 used -- more than double the 4-buy weekly cap. The budget check is currently BLOCKING new buys (red X on 'will it buy next scan?'), but the pace is a standing discipline flag independent of any single ticker.
